name: Law on Personal Data Protection (Zakon o zastiti podataka o licnosti)
summary: Serbia's GDPR-aligned data protection law was enacted as part of the country's EU accession process. It incorporates core GDPR principles including data protection by design, data portability, 72-hour breach notification, and mandatory data protection officers.
keyRequirements: Implement data protection by design and by default
Conduct data protection impact assessments for high-risk processing
Notify the Commissioner of data breaches within 72 hours
Appoint a data protection officer where required
Ensure lawful basis for processing (consent, contract, legal obligation, etc.)
Respect data subject rights including portability and erasure
penalties: Fines for legal entities range from RSD 50,000 to RSD 2,000,000 per violation (~EUR 17,000 maximum). Entrepreneurs face RSD 20,000 to RSD 500,000. Enforced by the Commissioner for Information of Public Importance and Personal Data Protection.
